methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $40M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 25 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 42.9x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
PAVE Space is a seed-stage space and aerospace company founded by Julie Böhning and Jérémy Marciacq, both co-founders of the Gruyère Space Program at EPFL. The company has raised $40M to date. The relevant comp universe consists of five public space and aerospace peers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and eight private peers including SpaceX, Rocket Lab, Blue Origin, Sierra Space, and Planet Labs.

Of the three valuation methods examined, only stage-progression analysis is applicable. This method uses the $40M total raised as a proxy for current enterprise value and applies a 2.5x multiplier typical of seed-to-next-stage progression in the space sector, yielding an implied valuation of $100M. Peer-median analysis could not be applied due to insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage comparables in the space and aerospace sector—only one adjacent-stage peer was identified against a minimum threshold of three. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples (median 42.9x across the five public tickers) is not executable without revenue disclosure, which private companies rarely provide.

The triangulated valuation range is $85M to $115M, with a median of $100M. This range reflects considerable uncertainty: it rests on a single methodological pillar, the applicability of a standard stage multiplier is unvalidated for PAVE Space's specific subsector and business model, and the absence of revenue data precludes cross-check via public market multiples. Key risks to valuation include technology commercialization timeline and customer concentration.
